title: Executive Order: Sanctions on Cuban Officials and Entities for Alleged Repression and Threats to U.S. National Security
summary: This Executive Order authorizes the imposition of economic sanctions against individuals and entities in Cuba designated as responsible for political repression and activities considered threatening to United States national security and foreign policy interests. The order is issued under authority granted by the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, the National Emergencies Act, section 212(f) of the Immigration and Nationality Act of 1952, and related statutory provisions. The executive action establishes a framework for identifying and sanctioning targeted parties through mechanisms available to the President under these legislative authorities.

title: Presidential Nominations Sent to the Senate for Confirmation
summary: A list of individuals nominated by the President for various federal positions, including Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary to Australia, Deputy Director of the Office of Management and Budget, and Assistant Secretary for Preparedness and Response at the Department of Health and Human Services, among other positions requiring Senate confirmation.

title: Presidential Nominations Submitted to Senate for Cabinet and Agency Leadership Positions
summary: The President submitted nominations to the Senate for various federal positions including Director of the Peace Corps, ambassadorial posts, and Inspector General positions within executive agencies. The nominations listed include Riley Barnes of Texas for Director of the Peace Corps, Rudolph Bauer of South Carolina as Ambassador to Belize, and Don Richard Berthiaume Jr. of Virginia as Inspector General of the Department of Justice, among other positions. These nominations require Senate confirmation before the nominees can assume their respective roles in the federal government.
